title: Cosmos Reactive Liquid — mic-reactive liquid surface with rippling metaball blobs and beat slosh
summary: A reactive liquid: a horizontal liquid surface whose height ripples and bulges from the spectrum, with glowing metaball blobs riding the crests and the whole body sloshing on the beat, finished with a glossy Cosmos sheen and specular crest sparkles. It reacts to the MICROPHONE live and gracefully falls back to a deterministic synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable or denied, plus a built-in synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero dependencies, runs fully offline.
usage_notes: RUN: open index.html and click "enable mic" to react to your voice or music; if no mic is granted it falls back to a built-in beat so it never goes blank. KEY TECHNIQUES: an AnalyserNode (fftSize 128) feeds a 64-bin spectrum + RMS level into render(spectrum, level, t); the surface height at each x samples the spectrum band plus travelling ripple sines, and a beat-driven slosh envelope heaves the whole sheet; metaball blobs are additive radial gradients placed on the surface, their radius pulsing with their spectrum bin and the slosh; a glossy sheen stroke and under-surface highlight give the Cosmos liquid look, with specular dots on local wave crests; synthSpectrum(u) is a seeded sum-of-sines with moving formant bumps plus a bass-weighted beat kick, deterministic and loop-closing, used for AUTO and live-fallback. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: NB (blob count), NX (surface resolution), amp (wave height), slosh power, baseLine, beat count (4).
